description_long: This object is a section of standard railway track, featuring two parallel steel rails mounted on a series of evenly spaced cross-ties, also known as sleepers. The rails are straight and run the full length of the object, supported by rectangular sleepers that are perpendicular to the rails. The sleepers appear to be made of a material resembling wood or concrete, providing stability and spacing for the rails. There is no visible ballast bed beneath the sleepers, and the track is not embedded in any pavement. The surface of the rails is smooth and metallic, while the sleepers have a matte, slightly rough finish. No signage, lights, or additional railway infrastructure is present.
description: A straight section of railway track with two parallel metal rails mounted on rectangular sleepers, lacking ballast or pavement embedding.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the two parallel rails and the series of cross-ties beneath, emphasizing the track's width.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the track, the alignment of the rails, and the evenly spaced sleepers.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view, similar to view 0, highlighting the parallel rails and cross-ties from the other end.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the length and structure of the track from the other side.
